---
title: SonarQube
description: Code quality and security analysis for 30+ languages with quality gates and dashboards.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Images:** `sonarqube:10.6-community` + `postgres:16`  
**Port:** `9000` (UI + API)  
**Data:** `sonarqube_data`, `sonarqube_extensions`, `sonarqube_logs`, `postgresql`, `postgresql_data`

## Introduction

[SonarQube Community Edition](https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarqube/) is a static analysis and security scanning platform for 30+ programming languages. It provides quality metrics, security hotspot detection, code smell identification, and quality gates that can block CI builds when standards aren't met.

This template bundles a dedicated PostgreSQL 16 database. The database is not exposed outside the Docker network.

## When to Use

- You want continuous code quality tracking across one or more repositories
- Your team needs security vulnerability detection beyond what your IDE provides
- You want to integrate quality gates into a Jenkins pipeline
- You're working in Java, C#, JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, or any of 30+ supported languages

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph LR
    Browser["Browser / CI Pipeline"] -->|":9000"| SQ
    Jenkins["Jenkins\n(scanner plugin)"] -->|"scan results\n:9000 API"| SQ
    subgraph SonarQube Stack
        SQ["SonarQube\n:9000"]
        PG["PostgreSQL 16\n(internal only)"]
        sq_data["sonarqube_data"]
        sq_ext["sonarqube_extensions"]
        sq_log["sonarqube_logs"]
        pg_data["postgresql_data"]
    end
    SQ <-->|"internal network"| PG
    SQ --- sq_data
    SQ --- sq_ext
    SQ --- sq_log
    PG --- pg_data
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- **Minimum 4 GB RAM** on the host (SonarQube's Elasticsearch is memory-intensive)
- **`vm.max_map_count=524288`** on Linux (see [Prerequisites](/prerequisites/#sonarqube-vmmax_map_count))

## Ports

| Port | Protocol | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `9000` | HTTP | SonarQube web UI and REST API |

Configurable via `SONARQUBE_PORT`. PostgreSQL is not exposed to the host.

## Volumes

| Volume | Contents |
|---|---|
| `sonarqube_data` | Analysis data and search indices |
| `sonarqube_extensions` | Installed plugins |
| `sonarqube_logs` | Application logs |
| `postgresql` | PostgreSQL configuration |
| `postgresql_data` | PostgreSQL data files |

## Configuration

```bash
cd sonarqube
cp .env.example .env
```

| Variable | Default | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `SONARQUBE_PORT` | `9000` | No | Web UI port |
| `SONAR_DB_PASSWORD` | _(none)_ | **Yes** | PostgreSQL password for the `sonar` user. No default — stack won't start without this |

```bash
# In sonarqube/.env
SONAR_DB_PASSWORD=choose-a-strong-password
```

## Workflow

```bash
# Start (ensure vm.max_map_count is set first on Linux)
docker compose up -d

# SonarQube takes ~60-90 seconds to start
docker compose logs -f sonarqube

# Open UI
open http://localhost:9000

# Default credentials (MUST change on first login):
# Username: admin
# Password: admin

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and remove all data (analyses, plugins, database)
docker compose down -v
```

## Use Cases

**Manual scan (Maven):**
```bash
mvn sonar:sonar \
  -Dsonar.projectKey=my-project \
  -Dsonar.host.url=http://localhost:9000 \
  -Dsonar.login=your-token
```

**Manual scan (SonarScanner CLI):**
```bash
docker run --rm \
  -e SONAR_HOST_URL=http://host:9000 \
  -e SONAR_LOGIN=your-token \
  -v "$(pwd):/usr/src" \
  sonarsource/sonar-scanner-cli
```

**Jenkins integration:**
Install the **SonarQube Scanner** plugin and the **Sonar Quality Gate** plugin. Configure the server URL in **Manage Jenkins → System → SonarQube servers**.

**Quality gate example:** Block deployments if code coverage drops below 80% or if new critical security hotspots are introduced.

## Security

:::danger[Default credentials]
SonarQube starts with `admin`/`admin`. Change this on first login. Anyone who can reach port 9000 with these credentials has admin access to all project analyses.
:::

## Docker Desktop Notes

SonarQube's memory requirements (4 GB minimum) may conflict with Docker Desktop's default memory limit. Increase it in **Docker Desktop → Settings → Resources → Memory**.

The `vm.max_map_count` setting is required even on Docker Desktop for Mac/Windows — apply it to the Docker Desktop VM as described in [Prerequisites](/prerequisites/#sonarqube-vmmax_map_count).

## Integration

### Nginx Proxy Manager

Uncomment the NPM block in `docker-compose.yml`, restart, and create a proxy host: `sonar.yourdomain.com → sonarqube:9000`.

### Jenkins

See the [Multi-Template Combinations](/combinations/#full-cicd-stack-jenkins--sonarqube--nginx-proxy-manager) page for the full Jenkins + SonarQube pipeline setup.
